area_anchor: Brikette commercial and arrival surfaces need rich-text-safe rendering and cleaner SEO canonicals
current_truth: The live Brikette funnel still had at least one support-surface copy leak where locale rich-text tokens such as <Link> rendered literally on /en/how-to-get-here. At the same time, sitemap and structured-data quality still lagged the revised funnel architecture: chooser pages emitted apartment markup, legacy redirect roots remained eligible for sitemap output, and homepage schema language coverage lagged the public locale surface.
next_scope_now: Implement a bounded Brikette micro-build that fixes support-route rich-text rendering, hardens static export audits against raw <Link>/<Strong> leaks, aligns private-accommodation JSON-LD with the chooser page role, removes redirect-only private roots from sitemap generation, and adds regression coverage for these conversion and SEO contracts.
adjacent_later: Extend rendered rich-text leakage audits to additional guide-detail surfaces if future locale content introduces new placeholder component tags
Normalize any remaining legacy-www health-check and canonical-host checks in release tooling once the unrelated workflow refactor settles
evidence_refs: operator-stated: /en/how-to-get-here exposed raw <Link>...</Link> copy instead of a real guest-facing link on the live funnel
why: The revised sales funnel is only trustworthy if support and arrival pages render cleanly in the shipped HTML and if search-facing metadata reflects the same information architecture the booking flow now uses. Raw rich-text placeholders on a live arrival page and stale chooser/apartment schema mismatches both damage trust before a guest reaches booking.

intended_outcome.statement: Brikette arrival and commercial support surfaces render localized rich-text safely in shipped HTML, the private-accommodation chooser exposes matching sitemap/JSON-LD contracts, and regression tests block future raw rich-text tag leaks.

completed_by.outcome: Arrival intro copy now renders <Trans>-backed links/strong text, static export auditing catches leaked rich-text tags on how-to-get-here routes, chooser JSON-LD matches the private booking IA, redirect-only private roots are excluded from sitemap generation, and regression tests cover these contracts.
